title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 3, 2026
headline: Two new methodology rulings — COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION (Alphabet/Google) and STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX (Bolivia) — are established on a zero-scored-movement cycle, as UnitedHealth Group accumulates an unprecedented three simultaneous sovereign enforcement actions converging on the same billing-conduct pattern, and Russia's June 2 mass strike on Kyiv delivers the most documented single active-perpetration event in the current assessment record.
summary: No scored movements this cycle. The benchmark's analytical value tonight is concentrated in three sub-threshold findings that extend the scoring architecture in consequential directions. UnitedHealth Group's sovereign-enforcement density reaches a new threshold: the Massachusetts AG's $100M MassHealth fraud suit (alleging a decade-long 2015–2025 SCO upcoding scheme, treble to ~$300M) and the Arizona AG's eight-insurer algorithmic price-fixing cartel suit both filed in the same window and converge with the already-priced DOJ MA-billing criminal probe — three simultaneous state and federal sovereign enforcement actions corroborating the same ACC/EMP profile. All remain newly-filed civil complaints; none are independently scorable pre-adjudication. But the FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED ruling is formally extended to sovereign-enforcement density: high density reinforces a near-floor profile that is already priced; the entity is a confirm-with-watch, not a confirm-and-move. Alphabet/Google's UK CMA world-first conduct ruling — forcing publisher opt-out from AI Overviews, AI Mode, Gemini, and Vertex without ranking penalty, plus fine-tuning opt-out and attribution requirements — introduces a new methodology ruling: COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION. A regulator-imposed remedy following an adverse finding is the inverse of a creditable self-initiated correction; the compelled-positive and the adverse-finding-negative offset to net-neutral. The entity sits at exactly the 40.0/41 Developing-Functional boundary; verified delivery of the remedy in approximately nine months is the upgrade trigger. Bolivia's terrorism and narcotics charges against union leaders amid a six-week insurrection produce the STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX ruling: genuine INT/ACC/BND/EMP pressure, discounted because a court suspended the lead terrorism warrant and the cabinet is split between dialogue and repression — contested in-flux repression with residual rule-of-law friction is scorable-but-sub-threshold. Russia's June 2 mass strike on Kyiv and Dnipro (73 missiles + 656 drones, 22 civilians killed including 2 children, 145 injured including 8 children, civilian infrastructure across 8 Kyiv districts, OHCHR-confirmed) reinforces the absolute 0.0 floor — an entity at 0.0 cannot move lower, but this is the largest single-strike documented civilian casualty event in the assessor's current record. Six entities confirm at the absolute floor: Russia, Sudan, South Sudan, Afghanistan, Myanmar, and xAI/Grok. The Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC has escalated to 381 cases and 64 deaths with confirmed Kampala spread; DRC confirms at 2.3 under the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT. Oracle is 12 days from the WARN Act June 15 deadline at 0.6 points above Critical.

topSignals.title: UnitedHealth Group 10.2 — Three Converging Sovereign Enforcement Actions; FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED Extended to Sovereign-Enforcement Density
topSignals.description: The Massachusetts AG filed a $100M civil suit on May 29 alleging that UnitedHealth's SCO upcoding program ran for a decade (2015–2025), systematically inflating MassHealth payments through diagnostic code manipulation with treble damages reaching approximately $300M. The Arizona AG followed on June 1 with a price-fixing cartel suit naming eight insurers including UnitedHealth plus data analytics firm MultiPlan, alleging algorithmic coordination to suppress provider reimbursement rates. Both join the existing DOJ MA-billing criminal probe — three simultaneous sovereign enforcement actions corroborating the same systemic billing-conduct pattern. The FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED ruling is formally extended to this class: sovereign-enforcement density (state and federal actions converging on a decade-long pattern) reinforces a near-floor ACC 1.125/EMP 1.25 profile rather than revealing previously-unpriced harm. The entity is already priced at deep Critical 10.2; even an aggressive modeled multi-dimension downgrade reaches only −2.0, sub-threshold. Confirm-with-watch; the strongest reassessment trigger of the cycle on any merits adjudication or settlement of the DOJ probe, the MA AG suit, or the AZ AG suit.

topSignals.title: Alphabet/Google 40.0 — UK CMA World-First Publisher Opt-Out Ruling; New COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION Ruling; Boundary at 40.0/41
topSignals.description: The UK Competition and Markets Authority issued on June 3 its world-first conduct ruling requiring Google to give publishers opt-out from AI Overviews, AI Mode, Gemini, and Vertex without ranking penalty, alongside fine-tuning opt-out and attribution requirements. A new methodology ruling — COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION — is established: where a regulatory body imposes a remedy following an adverse finding, the compelled remedy is the inverse of a creditable self-initiated correction (AB2/I2). It is the materialization of a documented harm — Google extracted publisher value until compelled to stop — not a compassion improvement. Future-positive credit is further discounted by the nine-month implementation runway, zero delivered effect, and UK-only scope. The compelled-positive and adverse-finding-negative offset to net-neutral; modeled generous credit of +0.9 falls below the threshold. Alphabet/Google sits at exactly the 40.0/41 Developing-Functional boundary — the only assessed entity where a new signal could plausibly drive upward movement. Verified delivery of the CMA opt-out and attribution remedy plus replication in EU or other jurisdictions constitutes the upgrade trigger.

topSignals.title: Russia 0.0 — June 2 Mass Strike on Kyiv and Dnipro; Largest Documented Single Civilian-Casualty Event in Current Assessment Record
topSignals.description: Russia's June 2 mass strike employed 73 missiles and 656 drones against Kyiv, Dnipro, and other cities, killing 22 civilians including 2 children and injuring 145 including 8 children. The attack struck civilian infrastructure across 8 Kyiv districts and was confirmed by OHCHR. Putin simultaneously rejected direct peace talks. Russia is published at the absolute 0.0 floor (harm-flag override, all dimensions 1.0); an entity at 0.0 cannot move lower. The June 2 event is the most extensively documented single active-perpetration strike in the assessor's current record — it reinforces rather than changes the designation.

topSignals.title: Bolivia 28.4 — Terrorism Charges Against Union Leaders and Masked-Vehicle Seizure; New STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX Ruling
topSignals.description: Following a six-week insurrection, Bolivia's government pursued terrorism and narcotics charges against COB chief Argollo and 24 union officials, carried out a masked-men unmarked-vehicle seizure of La Paz Fejuve secretary Apaza, and imposed 6-month preventive detention on union figures. These actions constitute genuine INT/ACC/BND/EMP pressure. A new methodology ruling — STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX — is established: where repression is concurrent with a contested political situation, a court suspension of the lead charge, and a cabinet divided between dialogue and further repression, the assessed downgrade is scorable-but-sub-threshold rather than constituting an ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT. The court's suspension of the Argollo terrorism warrant is the load-bearing rule-of-law variable. Modeled multi-dimension downgrade reaches −2.5, sub-threshold and non-band-crossing. Confirm-with-watch; converts on a documented mass-casualty crackdown, martial law with verified violations, or confirmed enforced disappearances.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 — Ebola Bundibugyo Escalates to 381 Cases, 64 Deaths; PHEIC; Kampala Transmission Confirmed
topSignals.description: The Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in DRC has reached 381 cases and 64 deaths with WHO PHEIC status declared. Confirmed transmission has reached Kampala, Uganda (population 3M+), with 15 Uganda cases documented. No approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain. Contact tracing covers only 45% of known chains. A $518M international response plan is active but post-USAID funding constraints limit mobilization capacity. DRC confirms at 2.3 under the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT ruling: the outbreak is an exogenous shock revealing priced state-response incapacity, absent documented deliberate obstruction. Documented deliberate state interference with the WHO response — denial of access, diversion of funds, suppression of case data — would break the overlay hold and constitute a scored downgrade trigger from the near-floor 2.3 baseline.

topSignals.title: Oracle 20.6 — WARN Act June 15 Deadline 12 Days Out; Boundary at 0.6 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window for approximately 30,000 affected workers closes June 15 — 12 days from tonight's cycle. The sign-or-forfeit materialization confirms the already-priced EMP 1.2 layoff conduct; no new scored adjustment is warranted from this event alone. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory (classifying remote workers to avoid WARN notification thresholds) and the Washington class action and New Jersey state WARN proceedings remain filed-but-unadjudicated. Oracle sits 0.6 points above the Critical boundary — the narrowest active margin in the Fortune 500 cluster. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the avoidance theory before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 — Scale-Floor Confirmed; 600+ Executions YTD; Arman Marefati at Imminent Risk
topSignals.description: Iran's execution rate in 2026 has reached 600+ year-to-date, including 39+ political prisoners and children on death row. Arman Marefati faces imminent execution risk. An 88-day internet shutdown was lifted May 26. The SCALE-FLOOR ruling holds at 2.5: the June execution surge deepens a single dominant extremity already priced in the composite and does not satisfy the multi-category threshold required for movement to the absolute 0.0 floor. Iran sits 2.5 points above the Critical-band floor; the scale-floor designation is the relevant anchor.

topSignals.title: xAI/Grok 0.0 — Pentagon $200M Classified Access; Documented Harmful Outputs; Floor Confirmed
topSignals.description: xAI/Grok's Pentagon classified-systems access agreement ($200M) is assessed under the MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT ruling: for xAI/Grok, classified military-platform access compounds an already-floored governance and safety profile — unlike for labs where civilian-oversight safeguards partially offset the contract. Senator Warren's active safety oversight inquiry and documented harmful outputs across multiple categories further reinforce the floor designation. The entity is published at 0.0 (harm-flag override, all dimensions 1.0); no downward movement is possible. The floor is confirmed with the most consequential state-actor safety-governance failure documented for any AI lab this cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Verified delivery of the CMA opt-out and attribution remedy (approximately nine months) plus replication in EU or another major jurisdiction constitutes the scored upgrade trigger. Adverse AI-Overviews antitrust finding in a parallel proceeding would produce downward pressure.
boundaryWatch.note: Alphabet/Google sits at the exact 40.0/41 Developing-Functional boundary. Tonight's COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling establishes that the CMA remedy is net-neutral this cycle: compelled-positive offsets adverse-finding-negative, with a nine-month runway and zero delivered effect. The boundary remains live; the upgrade condition is operationalized delivery, not announced intention.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day window closes June 15 — 12 days out. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action are the two active adjudication channels on the remote-reclassification avoidance theory.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 20.6 is 0.6 points above the Critical boundary — the narrowest active margin in the Fortune 500 cluster. The June 15 deadline is the most proximate known trigger date in the full benchmark dataset. The reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is pre-positioned for promotion to a scored category upon adjudication.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth — a favorable ruling credits the DoD-terms refusal as an I1 governance signal and triggers the Functional/Established crossing from 59.1.
boundaryWatch.note: Cycle 15. DC Circuit oral arguments remain divided with no ruling issued. Anthropic's holdout from the DoW classified-network access framework is a standing distinction from peer AI labs under the MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE candidate category.

emergingRisks.risk: UnitedHealth Group's three converging sovereign enforcement actions (DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, MA AG $100M MassHealth suit, AZ AG eight-insurer price-fixing cartel suit) represent the highest sovereign-enforcement density in the Fortune 500 cluster; the MA AG suit's decade-long pattern allegation is the most consequential single evidentiary claim in the corporate healthcare governance record.
emergingRisks.detail: The MA AG $100M MassHealth fraud suit (May 29) alleges systematic SCO upcoding from 2015–2025 with treble damages reaching approximately $300M. The AZ AG algorithmic price-fixing cartel suit (June 1) names UnitedHealth, seven co-insurer defendants, and MultiPlan. All three converging actions remain newly-filed civil or criminal complaints without merits adjudication. Conversion to a scored downgrade requires adjudication or settlement of any of the three actions.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle sits 0.6 points above Critical with the WARN Act June 15 sign-or-forfeit window closing in 12 days — the most proximate known hard deadline in the full benchmark dataset.
emergingRisks.detail: The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is pre-positioned for promotion to a scored category upon adjudication. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action are both active. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: The EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12 — nine days from tonight — creating new enforcement mechanisms for third-country return procedures that will be consequential for entities with standing non-refoulement profiles.
emergingRisks.detail: The Regulation imposes procedural and substantive constraints on EU-member and EU-adjacent return procedures. India (15.6 applied), Bolivia (28.4 confirmed-with-watch), and Romania (37.5 rotation-confirm) each carry standing non-refoulement or minority-return profiles. Any documented EU-facilitated deportation to a country where a third-country national faces persecution without individual assessment converts from watch to scored signal under the RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT anchor.

emergingRisks.risk: The Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC is entering its peak transmission window with confirmed Kampala urban spread, no approved vaccine, and 45% contact tracing — the most acute health emergency in the current benchmark dataset.
emergingRisks.detail: DRC at 381 cases/64 deaths; Uganda at 15 cases with Kampala confirmed. Post-USAID funding constraints limit global surge capacity at precisely the peak window. Documented deliberate state obstruction in either country breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold. DRC at 2.3 is near the absolute floor; an obstruction finding would trigger downward pressure.

emergingRisks.risk: Afghanistan's UNSC UNAMA mandate renewal vote on June 17 is the most significant near-term external accountability event for a floor-designated entity; Taliban's codified gender-persecution decrees (Decree No.12, Decree No.18) sustain the absolute floor independent of the vote's outcome.
emergingRisks.detail: Decree No.12 criminalizes domestic violence only on visible injury with a 15-day sentence; Decree No.18 imposes spousal-separation restrictions. Both are codified active perpetration. The June 17 UNSC vote is an external-process milestone that does not change the conduct score; a non-renewal of the mandate would reduce the accountability architecture without altering the entity's own conduct trajectory.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2 — 60 days from tonight — with no assessed AI lab having published compliance documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: Non-compliance fines can reach €35M or 7% of global annual turnover. OpenAI's comprehensive full assessment is overdue. The August 2 date is the first hard regulatory enforcement date the AI labs cluster has faced; compliance gap across the full cluster is accumulating.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan and South Sudan sustain a dual-country CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR designation with expanding IPC data: Sudan now has 2 NEW confirmed Darfur famine areas beyond the previously-documented set; South Sudan's IPC Phase 5 population is up 160% cycle-on-cycle.
emergingRisks.detail: Sudan: 33.7M requiring assistance; 19.5M acutely food insecure; 2 NEW confirmed Darfur famine areas. South Sudan: 7.8M (56%) acutely food insecure; 73,300 IPC Phase 5 (+160%); four counties at famine risk. Both at 0.0 floor. IPC Phase 5 area expansion is probable through the August lean-season peak at current funding levels.

methodologyNotes.name: COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established this cycle for Alphabet/Google. Where a regulatory authority imposes a remedy following an adverse finding — rather than the entity self-initiating a correction — the compelled remedy is the inverse of a creditable AB2/I2 self-correction signal. It is the materialization of a documented harm, not a compassion improvement. The future-positive credit for the remedy's delivery is discounted by the announced-not-operationalized standard (no delivered effect during the implementation window) and offset by the adverse-finding-negative (the finding confirms the harm was real and the entity did not self-correct). Net assessment is neutral. Alphabet/Google (UK CMA publisher opt-out ruling, nine-month implementation, zero delivered effect, UK-only) is the anchor case. An entity at this anchor can earn an upgrade on verified delivery plus jurisdiction replication, not on announcement.

methodologyNotes.name: STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established this cycle for Bolivia. Where a state pursues charges and detention of civil-society figures amid a political crisis, but the repression is contested through functioning rule-of-law mechanisms (e.g., a court suspends the lead terrorism warrant) and the executive posture is divided between dialogue and further repression, the assessed downgrade is scorable-but-sub-threshold rather than constituting an ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT. The distinguishing variables from full ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION are: (1) the conduct is in flux and not yet consolidated as a directed mass-harm regime; (2) a functioning court has contested the lead charge; (3) the executive is split rather than unified in repression. Bolivia (COB terrorism charges, court suspension of Argollo warrant, cabinet split, modeled downgrade −2.5, non-band-crossing) is the anchor case. Conversion to a full ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT occurs on documented mass-casualty crackdown, martial law with verified violations, or confirmed enforced disappearances.

methodologyNotes.name: FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED (sovereign-enforcement density extension)
methodologyNotes.description: Extended this cycle for UnitedHealth Group. The FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED ruling is formally extended to the class of sovereign-enforcement density: where multiple state and federal enforcement actions converge on the same conduct pattern within a single assessment window, the density itself is a documented feature of the risk profile that reinforces a near-floor composite, but the individual actions remain non-scorable pre-merits adjudication. The Massachusetts AG $100M MassHealth fraud suit, the Arizona AG eight-insurer price-fixing cartel suit, and the existing DOJ MA-billing criminal probe are the anchor case for this extension. Conversion to scored downgrade requires merits adjudication or settlement of any of the three actions.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-PERPETRATION-FLOOR
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for Russia, Myanmar, Afghanistan, and xAI/Grok. Where an entity is published at the absolute 0.0 floor (harm-flag override, all dimensions 1.0) and new adverse evidence documents further conduct in the same categories, the evidence reinforces the floor designation but cannot produce a proposal. Russia (June 2 mass strike, 22 civilians killed, OHCHR-confirmed), Myanmar (continued airstrikes; 5.2M displaced; ICC arrest-warrant application is external process, not conduct change), Afghanistan (codified gender persecution via Decrees 12 and 18), and xAI/Grok (Pentagon classified access; documented harmful outputs) are the reinforcing cases for this cycle.

methodologyNotes.name: CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for Sudan and South Sudan. Sudan confirms 2 NEW Darfur famine areas beyond the previously-documented set (33.7M needing assistance; 19.5M acutely food insecure; third year of SAF/RSF war). South Sudan confirms four counties at famine risk (7.8M/56% acutely food insecure; 73,300 IPC Phase 5 +160%; SAF/SPLM-IO access denial). Both entities sustain the active-perpetration 0.0 floor via state-attributable constrained humanitarian access.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for DRC. The Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak (381 cases/64 deaths; PHEIC; Kampala spread; no vaccine; 45% contact tracing) plus approximately 26.5M concurrent food insecure are an exogenous shock revealing already-priced state-response incapacity, absent documented deliberate state obstruction. The overlay holds at 2.3; documented deliberate state interference with the WHO response (access denial, fund diversion, data suppression) breaks the hold and constitutes a scored downgrade trigger.

methodologyNotes.name: OCCUPATION-ATTRIBUTION-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for Palestine. The fragile ceasefire, 700+ killed since October, and 77% food insecurity are substantially externally-attributable and occupying-power-attributable harm. The 25.0 baseline prices the assessed entity's own limited-capacity governance; externally-driven harm is not double-counted into a further downgrade of this entity. Changes in the assessed entity's own governance conduct (not the occupying power's) constitute the scoring trigger.

forwardTriggers.entity: EU Returns Regulation (Romania, India, Bolivia context)
forwardTriggers.trigger: EU Returns Regulation enters force; any documented EU-facilitated deportation to a country where a third-country national faces persecution without individual assessment constitutes a scored signal under the RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT anchor.

WARN Act last-working-day window closes for approximately 30,000 affected workers; any adjudicated finding on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in NJ state proceedings or the Washington class action constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.
UNSC UNAMA mandate renewal vote; non-renewal reduces accountability architecture; reversal of Decree No.12 or Decree No.18 constitutes the first improvement signal from the absolute floor.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July with confirmed Kampala transmission; documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect; fines up to €35M or 7% of global annual turnover; no assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation.
West and Central Africa lean season peaks; 2 NEW Darfur famine areas confirmed; Sudan humanitarian plan approximately 20% funded; further IPC Phase 5 area expansions probable through August.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the three converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, MA AG $100M MassHealth fraud suit, AZ AG eight-insurer price-fixing cartel suit — constitutes the leading corporate scoring trigger of the current cycle.

Verified delivery of the UK CMA publisher opt-out and attribution remedy (approximately nine months) plus replication in EU or another major jurisdiction constitutes the upgrade trigger from the 40.0/41 Developing-Functional boundary. Adverse AI-Overviews antitrust finding in a parallel proceeding produces downward pressure.

Documented mass-casualty crackdown, martial law with verified rights violations, or confirmed enforced disappearances convert the STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX sub-threshold to a scored INT/ACC/BND downgrade from 28.4. Negotiated settlement with release of detained union officials constitutes de-escalation.

Nationwide STF deportation scale-up or new mass-casualty refoulement event extending beyond the current evidence window; Supreme Court contempt enforcement on Gujarat demolitions; halt or reversal of the Union Home Ministry deportation directive.
DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth (Pentagon supply-chain-risk designation) — favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement.
Comprehensive full assessment overdue (50+ days); DoW classified-network agreement public terms; PBC conversion finalization; EU AI Act August 2 enforcement accumulating.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If a regulator imposes a remedy following an adverse finding — as the UK CMA did with Alphabet/Google on publisher opt-out — and the entity implements it fully within nine months, does the verified delivery of a compelled remedy constitute a creditable INT or EQU improvement under the COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling, or does the adverse-finding origin permanently discount the credit?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Alphabet/Google sits at exactly the 40.0/41 boundary; the question of how much weight verified delivery of a compelled remedy carries is the variable that determines whether the crossing holds.
